summary: Rate limiting and security headers implementation verified. All 14 backend tests passed. 5 security headers present on all responses (X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, Referrer-Policy, X-XSS-Protection). Rate limiting working on auth endpoints (5/minute) with correct 429 response format. Frontend pages /, /investors, /pilot, /telemetry all load correctly.

Rate_Limiting_Auth.429_response_format: PASS - Returns {"error": "Too many requests. Please try again later."}

Rate_Limiting_Telemetry.GET /api/status/platform: PASS - 60/minute limit (high limit, difficult to trigger)

Existing_APIs.GET /api/status/platform: PASS - Returns operational with metrics, cities, systems
Existing_APIs.POST /api/auth/login (bad creds): PASS - Returns 401 with error message (when not rate limited)

Frontend_Pages./ (Homepage): PASS - Loads with AI Safety Infrastructure content
Frontend_Pages./pilot: PASS - Pilot signup form loads with all fields
Frontend_Pages./telemetry: PASS - Live telemetry dashboard loads with platform status and events

critical_code_review_comments: Rate limiter correctly uses slowapi with get_remote_address for IP-based limiting
SecurityHeadersMiddleware properly adds all 5 required headers to every response
Rate limit exception handler returns correct JSON format for 429
Middleware order is correct: SecurityHeadersMiddleware before SlowAPIMiddleware
context_for_next_testing_agent: Rate limiting and security headers fully implemented. Auth endpoints (login/register/google) have 5/minute IP-based limit. Telemetry endpoints (platform/events/metrics) have 60/minute limit. Pilot signup has 10/hour limit. SOS trigger has 10/minute limit. All responses include 5 security headers. Note: In shared IP environments (like this preview), rate limits may trigger faster since all testers share the same IP bucket.
